items.correct: No - they are ellipses with the Sun at one focus (Kepler's First Law).
items.random_wrong: They are spirals slowly winding inward.
items.why: Most planetary orbits are only slightly elliptical, but they are ellipses, not exact circles.

items.q: Why does a planet speed up near the Sun?
items.correct: It sweeps equal areas in equal times, so it must move faster at perihelion.
items.misconception: The Sun's heat pushes it along faster.
items.random_wrong: Its mass increases near the Sun.
items.why: Kepler's Second Law links speed to position: nearer the Sun means a faster orbital speed.

items.q: What mainly causes Earth's ocean tides?
items.correct: The Moon's gravitational pull, which varies across the Earth.
items.misconception: The Sun's heat boiling the sea.
items.why: Tides come from the difference in the Moon's gravity across Earth, not from heat or spin alone.

items.q: Kepler's First Law states that planetary orbits are:
items.correct: Ellipses with the Sun at one focus
items.why: Correct answer: Ellipses with the Sun at one focus. “Perfect circles” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: A planet moves fastest in its orbit when it is:
items.why: Correct answer: Closest to the Sun (perihelion). “Farthest from the Sun” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Newton's law of gravitation says the force varies with separation as:
items.correct: Inversely with the square of distance
items.why: It's Inversely with the square of distance. “Proportional to distance” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: An orbit with an eccentricity of 0 is:
items.why: It's A perfect circle. “A parabola” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: What is the orbital eccentricity of a perfectly circular orbit?
items.why: Exact answer: 0 (no unit). Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
